topic: Socio-cultural Issues in Physical Activity and Sport
subject: pe

items.correct: Increases red blood cell production, improving oxygen-carrying capacity.
items.why: Students assume all PEDs work the same way. EPO is specifically for endurance - more red cells = more oxygen to working muscles. Anabolic steroids do something different (promote protein synthesis).

items.q: What type of joint is the knee?
items.why: Students confuse knee with hip. Both are in the leg, but the hip is ball-and-socket (multi-directional); the knee is a hinge (mostly flexion-extension). Ball-and-socket joints have more range.
Students confuse storage with circulation. Blood carries glucose; muscles and liver store glycogen (the storage form). Liver glycogen regulates blood sugar; muscle glycogen fuels local contraction.
It's 1972. “1956” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.
The answer examiners want is: 1995. “1985” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: Year football allowed professionalism in England?
items.why: Exact answer: 1885. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
